attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Find GraphQL route in internal-graphql api

content.text:
Two GraphQL services:

- **public-graphql**: endpoint at `/query` — `services/public-graphql/internal/router/router.go:43`
- **internal-graphql**: endpoint registered via `graphqlshared.InitHandlerService` — `services/internal-graphql/cmd/internal-graphql/api.go:255`

Which one are you interested in?

content.content:
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6	
7		cli "github.com/jawher/mow.cli"
8		accountapi "github.com/utilitywarehouse/account-platform-protobuf-model/gen/go/account/api/v1"
9		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/auth"
10		graphqlshared "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/graphql/shared"
11		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tbootstrap"
12		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tgrpc"
13		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tlog"
14		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/ttracing"
15		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tvars"
16		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset-blacklisting/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/handsetblacklisting"
17		lecpPb "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/lifecycle-events-comms-processor/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/lifecycleeventscommsprocessor"
18		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/porting-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/portingservice"
19		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/public-graphql/internal/graph"
20		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/public-graphql/internal/graph/model"
21		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/public-graphql/internal/router"
22		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/roaming-limit-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/roaminglimit"
23		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/sim-activation-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/simactivationservice"
24		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/sim-barring-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/simbarringservice"
25		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/uwos-go/iam"
26		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/uwos-go/iam/machine"
27		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/uwos-go/iam/pdp"
28		"google.golang.org/grpc"
29	)
30	
31	const (
32		appName = "public-graphql"
33		appDesc = "graphql gateway to mobile services, for customer facing apps"
34	)
35	
36	var gitHash string // populated at compile time
37	
38	func main() {
39		app := cli.App(appName, appDesc)
40	
41		opsAddress := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
42			Name:   "ops-address",
43			Desc:   "http ops address",
44			EnvVar: "OPS_ADDRESS",
45			Value:  ":8081",
46		})
47		httpAddress := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
48			Name:   "http-address",
49			Desc:   "The port to listen on for API GRAPHQL connections",
50			Value:  ":8080",
51			EnvVar: "HTTP_ADDRESS",
52		})
53		corsAllowOrigin := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
54			Name:   "cors-allow-origin",
55			Desc:   "A CSV of acceptable CORS origins",
56			EnvVar: "CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN",
57			Value:  "https://uw.je,http://localhost:8080",
58		})
59		corsAllowMethods := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
60			Name:   "cors-allow-methods",
61			Desc:   "Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value",
62			EnvVar: "CORS_ALLOW_METHODS",
63			Value:  "GET,POST",
64		})
65		corsAllowHeaders := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
66			Name:   "cors-allow-headers",
67			Desc:   "Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value",
68			EnvVar: "CORS_ALLOW_HEADERS",
69			Value:  "content-type,authorization",
70		})
71		playgroundEnabled := app.Bool(cli.BoolOpt{
72			Name:   "playground-enabled",
73			Desc:   "Enable token based authentication",
74			EnvVar: "PLAYGROUND_ENABLED",
75			Value:  false,
76		})
77		grpcBalancer := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
78			Name:   "grpc-balancer",
79			Desc:   "GRPC load balancer name [pick_first, round_robin, xds, grpclb] (defaults to round_robin)",
80			EnvVar: "GRPC_BALANCER",
81			Value:  "round_robin",
82		})
83		quotingAPIURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
84			Name:   "quoting-api-url",
85			Desc:   "Quouting API URL",
86			EnvVar: "QUOTING_API_URL",
87			Value:  "localhost:8090",
88		})
89		eeccContractRequestURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
90			Name:   "eecc-contract-request-url",
91			Desc:   "The eecc contract request GRPc URL",
92			EnvVar: "EECC_CONTRACT_REQUEST_URL",
93			Value:  "dns:///eecc-contract-request-api:8090",
94		})
95		myServicesAPIURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
96			Name:   "my-services-api-url",
97			Desc:   "My Services API URL",
98			EnvVar: "MY_SERVICES_API_URL",
99			Value:  "localhost:8091",
100		})
101		simSettingsServiceAPIURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
102			Name:   "sim-settings-service-api-url",
103			Desc:   "Sim Settings Service API URL",
104			EnvVar: "SIM_SETTINGS_SERVICE_API_URL",
105			Value:  "localhost:8091",
106		})
107		simReplacementServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
108			Name:   "sim-replacement-service-url",
109			Desc:   "Sim replacement service API URL",
110			EnvVar: "SIM_REPLACEMENT_SERVICE_URL",
111			Value:  "dns:///sim-replacement-service-api:8090",
112		})
113		topupServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
114			Name:   "topup-api-url",
115			Desc:   "Mobile TopUp API URL",
116			EnvVar: "MOBILE_TOPUP_API_URL",
117			Value:  "dns:///topup-api-app:8090",
118		})
119		portingOutServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
120			Name:   "porting-out-service-url",
121			Desc:   "Porting out service API URL",
122			EnvVar: "PORTING_OUT_SERVICE_URL",
123			Value:  "localhost:8092",
124		})
125		simActivationURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
126			Name:   "sim-activation-url",
127			Desc:   "The sim-activation-api GRPc URL",
128			EnvVar: "SIM_ACTIVATION_API_URL",
129			Value:  "dns:///sim-activation-api-app:8090",
130		})
131		portingServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
132			Name:   "porting-service-api-url",
133			Desc:   "Mobile porting service API URL",
134			EnvVar: "MOBILE_PORTING_SERVICE_API_URL",
135			Value:  "dns:///porting-service-app:8090",
136		})
137		handsetBlacklistingURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
138			Name:   "handset-blacklisting-url",
139			Desc:   "Handset blacklisting gRPC address.",
140			EnvVar: "HANDSET_BLACKLISTING_URL",
141			Value:  "dns:///handset-blacklisting-api:8090",
142		})
143		roamingLimitServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
144			Name:   "roaming-limit-service-url",
145			Desc:   "Roaming limit service gRPC address.",
146			EnvVar: "ROAMING_LIMIT_SERVICE_URL",
147		})
148		simBarringServiceURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
149			Name:   "sim-barring-service-url",
150			Desc:   "Sim barring service API URL",
151			EnvVar: "SIM_BARRING_SERVICE_URL",
152		})
153		lifecycleEventsCommsProcessorURL := *app.String(cli.StringOpt{
154			Name:   "lifecycle-events-comms-processor-url",
155			Desc:   "Lifecycle Events Comms Processor API URL",
156			EnvVar: "LIFECYCLE_EVENTS_COMMS_PROCESSOR_URL",
157		})
158		accountAPIURL := app.String(cli.StringOpt{
159			Name:   "account-api-url",
160			Desc:   "account-api-url",
161			EnvVar: "ACCOUNT_API_URL",
162		})
163	
164		eg, ctx, stop := tbootstrap.InitBootstrapGroup()
165		defer stop()
166	
167		tlog.RegisterDefaultLogger()
168		ttracing.MustRegisterTracing(ctx, appName, gitHash)
169	
170		app.Action = func() {
171			pdpClient, err := pdp.NewClient()
172			if err != nil {
173				tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "could not initialise pdp client")
174			}
175	
176			clientsConnections := graphqlshared.InitSharedClientConnections(ctx, graphqlshared.GRPCConnectionConfig{
177				LoadBalancer:              *grpcBalancer,
178				EeccContractRequestApiUrl: *eeccContractRequestURL,
179				QuotingClientUrl:          *quotingAPIURL,
180				MyServicesClientUrl:       *myServicesAPIURL,
181				SimSettingsClientUrl:      *simSettingsServiceAPIURL,
182				SimReplacementClientUrl:   *simReplacementServiceURL,
183				TopupApiClientUrl:         *topupServiceURL,
184				PortingOutClientUrl:       *portingOutServiceURL,
185			})
186	
187			dialOpts := tgrpc.GetDefaultDialOptions()
188			dialOpts.AddOptions(tgrpc.WithGRPCConnectionLoadBalancingPolicy(*grpcBalancer))
189	
190			simActivationConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *simActivationURL, dialOpts)
191			if err != nil {
192				tlog.WithError(err).
193					WithAttribute("address", *simActivationURL).
194					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
195			}
196			portingServiceConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *portingServiceURL, dialOpts)
197			if err != nil {
198				tlog.WithError(err).
199					WithAttribute("address", *portingServiceURL).
200					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
201			}
202			handsetBlacklistServiceConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *handsetBlacklistingURL, dialOpts)
203			if err != nil {
204				tlog.WithError(err).
205					WithAttribute("address", *handsetBlacklistingURL).
206					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
207			}
208			roamingLimitServiceConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *roamingLimitServiceURL, dialOpts)
209			if err != nil {
210				tlog.WithError(err).
211					WithAttribute("address", *roamingLimitServiceURL).
212					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
213			}
214	
215			simBarringServiceConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *simBarringServiceURL, dialOpts)
216			if err != nil {
217				tlog.WithError(err).
218					WithAttribute("address", *simBarringServiceURL).
219					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
220			}
221	
222			lifecycleEventsCommsProcessorConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, lifecycleEventsCommsProcessorURL, dialOpts)
223			if err != nil {
224				tlog.WithError(err).
225					WithAttribute("address", lifecycleEventsCommsProcessorURL).
226					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
227			}
228	
229			// A&I
230			iamMachine, err := machine.New()
231			if err != nil {
232				tlog.
233					WithError(err).
234					Fatal(ctx, "failed to create machine provider")
235			}
236			defer func(iamMachine machine.Provider) {
237				err := iamMachine.Close()
238				if err != nil {
239					tlog.
240						WithError(err).
241						Error(ctx, "failed to close machine provider")
242				}
243			}(iamMachine)
244	
245			accountDialOpts := tgrpc.GetDefaultDialOptions()
246			accountDialOpts.AddOptions(grpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(iam.UnaryClientInterceptor(iam.WithMachine(iamMachine))))
247			accountApiConn, err := tgrpc.InitGRPCConnection(ctx, *accountAPIURL, accountDialOpts)
248			if err != nil {
249				tlog.WithError(err).
250					WithAttribute("address", *accountAPIURL).
251					Fatal(ctx, "could not dial grpc server")
252			}
253	
254			resolver := &graph.Resolver{
255				Authorizer: auth.NewAuthorizer(pdpClient),
256				SharedResolver: graphqlshared.New(graphqlshared.GRPCClients{
257					QuotingClient:                clientsConnections.Clients.QuotingClient,
258					MyServicesClient:             clientsConnections.Clients.MyServicesClient,
259					EeccContractRequestApiClient: clientsConnections.Clients.EeccContractRequestApiClient,
260					SimSettingsClient:            clientsConnections.Clients.SimSettingsClient,
261					TopUpApiClient:               clientsConnections.Clients.TopUpApiClient,
262					PortingOutServiceClient:      clientsConnections.Clients.PortingOutServiceClient,
263					SimReplacementServiceClient:  clientsConnections.Clients.SimReplacementServiceClient,
264				}),
265				QuotingClient:                         clientsConnections.Clients.QuotingClient,
266				MyServicesClient:                      clientsConnections.Clients.MyServicesClient,
267				EeccContractRequestApiClient:          clientsConnections.Clients.EeccContractRequestApiClient,
268				SimSettingsClient:                     clientsConnections.Clients.SimSettingsClient,
269				TopupApiClient:                        clientsConnections.Clients.TopUpApiClient,
270				PortingOutClient:                      clientsConnections.Clients.PortingOutServiceClient,
271				SimReplacementClient:                  clientsConnections.Clients.SimReplacementServiceClient,
272				SimActivationApiClient:                simactivationservice.NewServiceClient(simActivationConn),
273				PortingServiceClient:                  portingservice.NewServiceClient(portingServiceConn),
274				HandsetBlacklistingServiceClient:      handsetblacklisting.NewHandsetBlacklistingApiClient(handsetBlacklistServiceConn),
275				RoamingLimitServiceClient:             roaminglimit.NewRoamingLimitApiClient(roamingLimitServiceConn),
276				SimBarringServiceClient:               simbarringservice.NewServiceClient(simBarringServiceConn),
277				LifecycleEventCommsProcessorApiClient: lecpPb.NewLifecycleEventsCommsProcessorApiClient(lifecycleEventsCommsProcessorConn),
278				AccountApiClient:                      accountapi.NewAccountServiceClient(accountApiConn),
279			}
280	
281			httpRouter := router.NewRouter(router.Opts{
282				AppName:           appName,
283				CorsAllowOrigin:   *corsAllowOrigin,
284				CorsAllowMethods:  *corsAllowMethods,
285				CorsAllowHeaders:  *corsAllowHeaders,
286				PlaygroundEnabled: *playgroundEnabled,
287			}, graphqlshared.InitHandlerService(model.NewExecutableSchema(model.Config{Resolvers: resolver})))
288	
289			httpServer := tbootstrap.NewHttpServer(*httpAddress, httpRouter)
290	
291			opsServer := tbootstrap.InitOpsServer(ctx, tbootstrap.OpsServerOpts{
292				AppName:        appName,
293				AppDescription: appDesc,
294				Revision:       gitHash,
295				Addr:           *opsAddress,
296				Checkers: []tbootstrap.CheckerBuilder{
297					&tbootstrap.HttpChecker{
298						CheckName:                    tvars.CheckGraphQlServerLabel,
299						HttpServerHealthcheckAddress: fmt.Sprintf("%s/health", *httpAddress),
300					},
301				},
302			})
303	
304			err = tbootstrap.StartBootstrapGroup(eg,
305				tbootstrap.WithOpsServer(ctx, opsServer),
306				tbootstrap.WithWaitableHttpServer(ctx, httpServer, tvars.CheckGraphQlServerLabel),
307				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.QuotingApiClientConn),
308				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.MyServicesClientConn),
309				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.EeccContractRequestApiConn),
310				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.SimSettingsClientConn),
311				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.TopupApiClientConn),
312				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.PortingOutClientConn),
313				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, clientsConnections.Connections.SimReplacementClientConn),
314				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, simActivationConn),
315				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, handsetBlacklistServiceConn),
316				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, portingServiceConn),
317				tbootstrap.WithGRPCClient(ctx, simBarringServiceConn),
318			)
319	
320			if err != nil {
321				tlog.WithError(err).Error(ctx, "error returned from error group")
322			}
323		}
324		if err := app.Run(os.Args); err != nil {
325			tlog.WithError(err).Fatal(ctx, "unable to run app")
326		}
327	}
328

1	package router
2	
3	import (
4		"net/http"
5	
6		"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler"
7		"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/playground"
8		"github.com/gorilla/mux"
9	
10		graphqlmiddleware "github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/graphql/middleware"
11		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/pkg/libdry/tmiddleware"
12	
13		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/uwos-go/iam"
14		"go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/github.com/gorilla/mux/otelmux"
15	)
16	
17	type Opts struct {
18		AppName           string
19		CorsAllowOrigin   string
20		CorsAllowMethods  string
21		CorsAllowHeaders  string
22		PlaygroundEnabled bool
23	}
24	
25	var (
26		playgroundPath = "/playground"
27		queryEndpoint  = "/query"
28		healthCheck    = "/health"
29	)
30	
31	func NewRouter(opts Opts, handler *handler.Server) *mux.Router {
32		corsMiddleware := graphqlmiddleware.NewCorsMiddleware(opts.CorsAllowOrigin, opts.CorsAllowMethods, opts.CorsAllowHeaders)
33		router := mux.NewRouter()
34	
35		router.Use(corsMiddleware.Handle)
36		router.Use(otelmux.Middleware(opts.AppName))
37		router.Use(tmiddleware.AuditChannelAwareHTTPMiddleware)
38	
39		if opts.PlaygroundEnabled {
40			router.HandleFunc(playgroundPath, playground.Handler("GraphQL playground", queryEndpoint))
41		}
42	
43		router.Path("/query").Handler(iam.HTTPHandler(false)(handler))
44	
45		// healthcheck
46		router.Path(healthCheck).Handler(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
47			w.WriteHeader(200)
48		}))
49	
50		return router
51	}
52

lastPrompt: how does that public service work?

content.text:
Here's the summary:

**It's a GraphQL gateway.** The frontend hits `/query`, and the service fans out to ~10 downstream gRPC services.

**Request flow:**
1. Request hits `/query` → IAM auth middleware validates the token
2. CORS + tracing middleware applied
3. `gqlgen` resolves the query via `graph.Resolver`

**The resolver holds gRPC clients for:**
- `my-services-api` (subscriptions/plans)
- `quoting-api`
- `eecc-contract-request-api`
- `sim-settings-service`
- `sim-activation-api`
- `sim-replacement-service`
- `topup-api`
- `porting-service`
- `handset-blacklisting`
- `roaming-limit-service`
- `sim-barring-service`
- `lifecycle-events-comms-processor`
- `account-api`

The actual query/mutation logic lives in `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/schema.resolvers.go`.

1	//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate

22		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/porting-out-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/portingoutservice"
23		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/porting-service/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/portingservice"

38	// This file will not be regenerated automatically.
39	//
40	// It serves as dependency injection for your app, add any dependencies you require here.
41	
42	type Resolver struct {
43		Authorizer                            auth.Authorizer
44		SharedResolver                        graphqlshared.SharedResolver
45		EeccContractRequestApiClient          eecc_contract_request_api.ServiceClient
46		QuotingClient                         quotingapi.ServiceClient
47		MyServicesClient                      myservicesapi.ServiceClient
48		SimSettingsClient                     simsettingsservice.ServiceClient
49		SimReplacementClient                  simreplacementservice.ServiceClient
50		TopupApiClient                        topupapi.ServiceClient
51		PortingOutClient                      portingoutservice.ServiceClient
52		SimActivationApiClient                simactivationservice.ServiceClient
53		PortingServiceClient                  portingservice.ServiceClient
54		HandsetBlacklistingServiceClient      handsetblacklisting.HandsetBlacklistingApiClient
55		RoamingLimitServiceClient             roaminglimit.RoamingLimitApiClient
56		SimBarringServiceClient               simbarringservice.ServiceClient
57		LifecycleEventCommsProcessorApiClient lecpPb.LifecycleEventsCommsProcessorApiClient
58		AccountApiClient                      accountapi.AccountServiceClient
59	}
60	
61	const (
62		resource     = "uw.mobile.v1.public_graphql"
63		resourceID   = "telecom_mobile_public_gql"
64		AuditChannel = "Web"
65	)
66	
67	func (r *Resolver) authorize(ctx context.Context, action cerbos.Action) (*auth.AuthCustomerResponse, error) {
68		return r.Authorizer.AuthorizeCustomer(ctx, action, resource, resourceID)
69	}
70	
71	func (r *Resolver) isServiceOwnedByCustomer(ctx context.Context, auth auth.AuthCustomerResponse, serviceID string) (bool, error) {
72		resp, err := r.MyServicesClient.GetServices(ctx, &myservicesapi.GetServicesRequest{
73			CustomerId: auth.AccountNumber,
74		})
75	
76		if err != nil {
77			return false, fmt.Errorf("unable to verify service ownership")
78		}
79	
80		for _, svc := range resp.GetServices() {
81			if serviceID == svc.GetMobileService().GetServiceId() {
82				return true, nil
83			}
84		}
85	
86		return false, nil
87	}
88	
89	func (r *Resolver) toDomainMobilePortingRequest(details *sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutInput) (*porting_out_events.MobilePortingRequest, error) {
90		if details == nil {
91			return nil, errors.New("pac request cannot be nil")
92		}
93		timestamp := timestamppb.Now()
94		if details.Time != nil {
95			var err error
96			timestamp, err = graphql.UnmarshalDateTimeRFC3339(details.Time)
97			if err != nil {
98				return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to unmarshal time: %w", err)
99			}
100		}
101	
102		toModelSource := func(val sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSource) porting_out_events.Source {
103			switch val {
104			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSource1p:
105				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_1P
106			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSourceClubhouse:
107				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_CLUBHOUSE
108			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSourceCrm:
109				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_CRM
110			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSourceSmpp:
111				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_SMPP
112			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSourceUnknown:
113				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_UNKNOWN
114			default:
115				return porting_out_events.Source_SOURCE_UNKNOWN
116			}
117		}
118	
119		toPortingType := func(portType sharedmodel.MobilePortingType) porting_out_events.Type {
120			switch portType {
121			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeStac:
122				return porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_STAC
123			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypePac:
124				return porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_PAC
125			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeInfo:
126				return porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_INFO
127			case sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeUnknown:
128				return porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_UNKNOWN
129			default:
130				return porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_UNKNOWN
131			}
132		}
133	
134		ID := ""
135		if details.ID != nil {
136			ID = *details.ID
137		}
138		correlationToken := ""
139		if details.CorrelationToken != nil {
140			correlationToken = *details.CorrelationToken
141		}
142		source := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutSourceSourceUnknown
143		if details.Source != nil {
144			source = *details.Source
145		}
146	
147		return &porting_out_events.MobilePortingRequest{
148			Id:               ID,
149			Cli:              details.Cli,
150			Type:             toPortingType(details.Type),
151			Source:           toModelSource(source),
152			Time:             timestamp,
153			CorrelationToken: correlationToken,
154		}, nil
155	}
156	
157	func (r *Resolver) toPacResponse(res *porting_out_events.MobilePortingResponse) *sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutState {
158		authCodeExpiry := utils.TimestampbbToString(res.GetAuthCodeExpiry())
159		requestTime := utils.TimestampbbToString(res.GetRequestTime())
160		time := utils.TimestampbbToString(res.GetTime())
161	
162		toModelStatus := func(val porting_out_events.AuthCodeStatus) *sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatus {
163			switch val {
164			case porting_out_events.AuthCodeStatus_AUTH_CODE_STATUS_GENERATED:
165				st := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatusAuthCodeStatusGenerated
166				return &st
167			case porting_out_events.AuthCodeStatus_AUTH_CODE_STATUS_UNKNOWN:
168				st := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatusAuthCodeStatusUnknown
169				return &st
170			case porting_out_events.AuthCodeStatus_AUTH_CODE_STATUS_CONFLICT:
171				st := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatusAuthCodeStatusUw // not entirely sure about this one
172				return &st
173			case porting_out_events.AuthCodeStatus_AUTH_CODE_STATUS_RETRIEVED:
174				st := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatusAuthCodeStatusRetrieved
175				return &st
176			default:
177				st := sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutAuthCodeStatusAuthCodeStatusUnknown
178				return &st
179			}
180		}
181	
182		toModelBusinessUnit := func(bu porting_out_events.BusinessUnit) *sharedmodel.MobileBusinessUnit {
183			switch bu {
184			case porting_out_events.BusinessUnit_BUSINESS_UNIT_UNKNOWN:
185				b := sharedmodel.MobileBusinessUnitBusinessUnitUnknown
186				return &b
187			case porting_out_events.BusinessUnit_BUSINESS_UNIT_1P:
188				b := sharedmodel.MobileBusinessUnitBusinessUnit1p
189				return &b
190			case porting_out_events.BusinessUnit_BUSINESS_UNIT_UW:
191				b := sharedmodel.MobileBusinessUnitBusinessUnitUw
192				return &b
193			default:
194				b := sharedmodel.MobileBusinessUnitBusinessUnitUnknown
195				return &b
196			}
197		}
198	
199		etfAmount := res.GetEtf().GetAmount()
200		etfCurrencyCode := res.GetEtf().GetCurrencyCode()
201		authCode := res.GetAuthCode()
202		cli := res.GetCli()
203		correlationToken := res.GetCorrelationToken()
204		id := res.GetId()
205		rawCli := res.GetRawCli()
206	
207		toModelType := func(portType porting_out_events.Type) *sharedmodel.MobilePortingType {
208			switch portType {
209			case porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_STAC:
210				pt := sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeStac
211				return &pt
212			case porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_PAC:
213				pt := sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypePac
214				return &pt
215			case porting_out_events.Type_TYPE_UNKNOWN:
216				pt := sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeUnknown
217				return &pt
218			default:
219				pt := sharedmodel.MobilePortingTypePortTypeUnknown
220				return &pt
221			}
222		}
223	
224		return &sharedmodel.MobilePortingOutState{
225			AuthCode:         &authCode,
226			AuthCodeExpiry:   &authCodeExpiry,
227			AuthCodeStatus:   toModelStatus(res.GetAuthCodeStatus()),
228			BusinessUnit:     toModelBusinessUnit(res.GetBusinessUnit()),
229			Cli:              &cli,
230			CorrelationToken: &correlationToken,
231			Etf: &sharedmodel.MobileMoney{
232				Amount:   etfAmount,
233				Currency: etfCurrencyCode,
234			},
235			ID: &id,
236			PrepayBalance: &sharedmodel.MobileMoney{
237				Amount:   res.GetEtf().GetAmount(),
238				Currency: res.GetEtf().GetCurrencyCode(),
239			},
240			RawCli:      &rawCli,
241			RequestTime: &requestTime,
242			Time:        &time,
243			Type:        toModelType(res.GetType()),
244		}
245	}
246	
247	func (r *Resolver) map3DSActionResponse(action *topupapi.RequiredAction) *model.MobileTopupRequiredAction {
248		if action == nil {
249			return nil
250		}
251	
252		return &model.MobileTopupRequiredAction{
253			TransactionData:          new(string(action.GetTransactionData()[:])),
254			ExternalTransactionToken: action.GetExternalTransactionToken(),
255		}
256	}
257	func (r *Resolver) mapTopupFailureCode(failureCode *topupapi.FailureCode) *string {
258		var output *string
259	
260		failureMessage := map[topupapi.FailureCode]string{
261			topupapi.FailureCode_FAILURE_PAYMENT_REFUSED:            "Payment failed. For more information please contact your bank.",
262			topupapi.FailureCode_FAILURE_PAYMENT_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED: "Invalid payment method. Please try a different card.",
263			topupapi.FailureCode_FAILURE_ADDRESS_CHECK_FAILED:       "Error: Payment details do not match.",
264			topupapi.FailureCode_FAILURE_3DS_REFUSED:                "Authentication failed. Please try again.",
265			topupapi.FailureCode_FAILURE_INVALID_PAYMENT_METHOD:     "Invalid card. Please check your details or try a different card.",
266		}
267	
268		if failureCode != nil {
269			msg := failureMessage[*failureCode]
270			output = &msg
271		}
272		return output
273	}
274	
275	func (r *Resolver) getOverrideAddress(req model.MobileSimReplacementRequest) *customer_events.BillCustomerAddress {
276		if req.OverrideAddressLine1 == nil &&
277			req.OverrideAddressLine2 == nil &&
278			req.OverrideAddressLine3 == nil &&
279			req.OverrideAddressLine4 == nil &&
280			req.OverrideCounty == nil &&
281			req.OverridePostCode == nil {
282			return nil
283		}
284	
285		return &customer_events.BillCustomerAddress{
286			AddressLine_1: utils.PtrToString(req.OverrideAddressLine1),
287			AddressLine_2: utils.PtrToString(req.OverrideAddressLine2),
288			AddressLine_3: utils.PtrToString(req.OverrideAddressLine3),
289			AddressLine_4: utils.PtrToString(req.OverrideAddressLine4),
290			County:        utils.PtrToString(req.OverrideCounty),
291			PostCode:      utils.PtrToString(req.OverridePostCode),
292		}
293	}

lastPrompt: how does the fanning out work?>

content.text:
The pattern is simple and consistent across every resolver:

1. **Authorize** — every resolver starts with `r.authorize(ctx, cerbos.SOME_ACTION)`, which calls PDP/Cerbos to check the customer's IAM token
2. **Ownership check** (mutations/sensitive queries) — calls `my-services-api` to confirm the service being acted on belongs to the authenticated customer
3. **Single gRPC call** — calls exactly one downstream service and maps the response to GQL types

There's no parallel fan-out — each GraphQL field resolves to exactly one gRPC call. The "fanning out" is just that different fields route to different services; a single GraphQL request that queries multiple fields will trigger multiple sequential gRPC calls as gqlgen resolves each field independently.

Some resolvers delegate to `r.SharedResolver` — that's a shared package (`pkg/graphql/shared`) used by both public and internal GraphQL services to avoid duplication.

message.content: do we have a handset endpoint?

content.text:
Yes — `MobileHandsetInformation` at `schema.resolvers.go:761`. It takes a list of IMEI numbers, validates them, and returns whether they're valid. It doesn't actually call a downstream service — it just runs local IMEI validation and echoes back the numbers.

There's also `MobileBlacklistHandset` (mutation, line 375) which calls `handset-blacklisting-service` to blacklist by IMEI.
